text: to Settings > Analytics . Navigate to Settings > Analytics . Scroll down to the Spend section. Scroll down to the Spend section. Click the "Export Spend Report" button. Click the "Export Spend Report" button. Select a time period: MTD, Last Month, Last 90 Days, or Custom. Select a time period: MTD, Last Month, Last 90 Days, or Custom. If you select "Custom," choose your start and end dates. You can go back up to 90 days, and the most recent data available is from yesterday. If you select "Custom," choose your start and end dates. You can go back up to 90 days, and the most recent data available is from yesterday. Click "Download." Click "Download." What's included in the report? Each row in the CSV represents a specific person's usage of a specific model, with spend summed across the entire date range selected for the export. The report includes the following fields: User's email User's email Account UUID Account UUID Product (such as Chat, Claude Code, Cowork, or Office Agents) Office Agents aggregates usage from the Claude add-ins for Excel, PowerPoint, and Word. Product (such as Chat, Claude Code, Cowork, or Office Agents) Office Agents aggregates usage from the Claude add-ins for Excel, PowerPoint, and Word. Office Agents aggregates usage from the Claude add-ins for Excel, PowerPoint, and Word. Model and model family Model and model family Request count ( total_requests ) The count of individual API calls made to Claude. Each time an app or user sends a message and gets a response, that counts as a request. Request count ( total_requests ) The count of individual API calls made to Claude. Each time an app or user sends a message and gets a response, that counts as a request. The count of individual API calls made to Claude. Each time an app or user sends a message and gets a response, that counts as a request. Prompt tokens ( total_prompt_tokens ) The number of tokens consumed by the input side of each request.
